Alerts

Revenue is down 96% versus June.
Break-even threshold has not been reached (3% of the way there).
This month is currently running at a deficit.

Growth Analysis Which category to push, based on margin per sash and actual volume sold this month

Finance Head Briefing Auto-generated from this month's recorded numbers

As of 5 July 2026, July has recorded GH₵480 in sales against GH₵1,154 in expenses, a deficit of GH₵674. That's behind the GH₵8,000 profit target set for the month.

Revenue is currently down 96% versus June, and the business has covered 3% of the revenue needed to break even this month. Outstanding receivables stand at GH₵1,240.

Financial health score is 67/100. Gross margin is -138.5% and cash on hand covers roughly 2.6 months of average expenses.

Recommended actions

  • Investigate the deficit — check whether a large one-off expense or a slow sales week is the driver, and confirm cash is not running critically low.
  • Push sales toward the break-even threshold before mid-month to keep pace with target.
  • Revenue has slowed noticeably versus June — worth a quick look at whether it's seasonal or a pipeline issue.
  • Investor repayment to Esther Adjetey Astwei (GH₵3,300) is due in ~26 days — plan the cash for it.

Products Add every sash line you sell — selling price and cost price only

ProductSelling PriceCost PriceUnits Sold (this month)Profit / Unit
GH₵36.40
GH₵29.00
Cost price = what it costs you to make one sash (weaving/embroidery + packaging + overhead). Units sold sets each product's weight in the blended break-even below — it doesn't need to be exact, your best estimate for the month is fine.

Fixed Costs Costs that don't change with how many sashes you sell

Forecast Assumptions

—
Growth rates default to the compound monthly trend implied by January vs. June 2026 actuals (smoother than averaging noisy month-to-month swings). Adjust either rate to model a faster or slower scenario — the table and chart update live.

Record a Payment

Date defaults to today but can be backdated for late entries. Achiever and order details are tracked in the CRM, not here. Made a mistake, or an achiever completed a part-payment? Edit the Status or Amount directly in the log below and click Save, or delete the row entirely. Category and quantity power the blended target-profit calculation and growth analysis — tag what you can, older entries left as Uncategorized are simply excluded from that math.

Add Staff

Bonus rule: 20% of basic pay if the performance threshold is met, GH₵0 if not. SSNIT/PAYE deductions plug into the Deductions field for now — statutory calculation is on the roadmap.

Add an Asset

Depreciation method is straight-line across the board: Annual Depreciation = Cost ÷ Useful Life. Only the Motor Vehicle has a recorded cost in your ledger so far (GH₵10,900, purchased Jan 2026) — the camera, tripod, mic, and phone are tracked as accounts but no cost was ever logged, so add their real purchase costs here when you have them.
